title: Festuca simpliciuscula
intro: Festuca simpliciuscula, also known as blue fescue grass or mountain fescue, is a perennial grass species native to mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Festuca simpliciuscula include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Festuca simpliciuscula is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in salves and creams to support skin health and wound healing.
taxonomy: Festuca simpliciuscula is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Festuca and is a part of the subfamily Pooideae. Within the genus Festuca, F. simpliciuscula is often classified as a member of the section Calliergon or the section Festuca. The species is native to the Arctic regions of the Northern Hemisphere, where it grows in cold, wet environments. As a member of the Poaceae family, F. simpliciuscula is a monocotyledonous plant.
